sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
Global FX Overview — Asian Session

Dollar weakness dominates Asian trading as risk appetite recovers amid growing speculation that major central banks may be nearing the end of their tightening cycles. The greenback's broad-based decline reflects renewed investor confidence in emerging market assets and commodity currencies, with particular strength evident across the antipodean complex. Markets are positioning for potential Fed dovishness ahead of key economic data releases, while carry trade strategies gain renewed momentum as volatility subsides.

Currency Strength

NZD leads the pack with stellar 1.16% intraday gains, followed by AUD at 0.49% as commodity flows and risk-on sentiment drive antipodean outperformance. EUR advances 0.24% on hawkish ECB speculation, while GBP gains 0.17% despite ongoing fiscal concerns. USD weakens across the board with -0.448 strength index reading. CHF underperforms at -0.50% as safe-haven demand wanes, while JPY shows mixed performance amid intervention speculation around 159.30 levels.

FX Majors

EUR/USD at 1.1646 breaks above key 1.1630 resistance, targeting 1.1680 psychological level. ECB hawkish rhetoric supports euro strength with bullish bias intact. GBP/USD at 1.3439 consolidates recent gains despite settlement weakne

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
ss of -0.31%. UK data dependency maintains cautious optimism. USD/JPY at 159.3020 remains precarious near intervention zones. BoJ rhetoric intensifies as pair tests multi-decade highs with bearish dollar bias emerging. AUD/USD at 0.7168 extends recovery above 0.7150, targeting 0.7200 on commodity strength and China optimism. USD/CHF at 0.7839 breaks below 0.7850 support, targeting 0.7820 as safe-haven demand diminishes. USD/CAD at 1.3786 weakens on oil price recovery and dovish Fed expectations, eyeing 1.3750 support. NZD/USD at 0.5961 surges through 0.5950 resistance, targeting 0.6000 milestone on RBNZ hawkishness.

FX Crosses

EUR/JPY at 185.4480 remains constructive above 185.00 support as ECB hawkishness counters yen strength. NZD/JPY at 94.9070 leads cross performance with 0.98% intraday gains, benefiting from dual central bank divergence. GBP/JPY at 214.0730 consolidates at elevated levels despite flat intraday performance. AUD/JPY at 114.1410 extends gains on risk-on flows and commodity strength. EUR/GBP at 0.8662 stabilizes as both currencies advance against the dollar.

Flows and Positioning

• Renewed carry trade activity favoring high-yielding currencies as volatility compression continues
• Asian real money accounts actively selling USD against commo

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
dity currencies on dip-buying strategies  
• Hedge fund positioning shows increased short USD exposure ahead of US inflation data
• Month-end rebalancing flows supporting EUR and GBP against dollar weakness

Trade Ideas Intraday

LONG: NZD/USD targeting 0.6000 on RBNZ hawkish momentum and technical breakout. AUD/JPY exploiting risk-on sentiment and commodity strength toward 115.00. EUR/CHF benefiting from ECB divergence and safe-haven unwinding toward 0.9150.

SHORT: USD/CHF on dollar weakness and risk appetite recovery targeting 0.7820. USD/CAD on oil price recovery and Fed dovish expectations toward 1.3750. EUR/GBP fade on relative monetary policy convergence targeting 0.8640.

Macro Drivers Today

US durable goods orders and consumer confidence data could extend dollar weakness if disappointing. ECB speakers Lagarde and Schnabel may reinforce hawkish stance. Australian private capex data expected to support AUD strength. China PMI data overnight showed marginal improvement supporting risk sentiment.

Risks

BoJ intervention remains imminent above 159.50 in USD/JPY, potentially triggering broad yen strength. Geopolitical tensions in Eastern Europe could reverse risk-on flows rapidly. US data surprises to upside may halt dollar weakness and unwind carry trades.
